mapping.a_element: gauge-invariant coherent states on graphs with SU(2) symmetry
mapping.b_element: dynamical graphs on N vertices with permutation symmetry at high energy

mapping.a_element: gauge-invariant phase space and its singularities (degenerate gauge orbits)
mapping.b_element: low-energy phase where permutation symmetry is broken and the graph becomes ordered/local

mapping.a_element: Gauss-peaked overlap of coherent states in non-degenerate regions
mapping.b_element: thermodynamic stability of the low-energy local graph phase under perturbations

mapping.a_element: geometry of the gauge-invariant configuration space influencing semiclassical properties
mapping.b_element: emergent locality and spatial geometry from the graph's low-energy phase

brief_justification: While both papers involve graphs and symmetry breaking, Paper A focuses on the mathematical structure of gauge-invariant coherent states and their semiclassical properties in a quantum gravity context, whereas Paper B models emergent locality and spatial geometry through thermodynamic phases of dynamical graphs. The core mechanisms—coherent state overlaps in gauge theory vs. phase transitions in graph connectivity—are distinct, despite superficial similarities in graph-based representations.
